text: Is it more important for a long-term partner to share your core values, or your sense of humor?
If you could know the exact day and time a friend was going to betray you, would you want to know?
Is unconditional love a healthy ideal, or is all healthy love conditional on behavior?
Would you rather have a partner who is completely honest even when it hurts, or one who uses white lies to preserve your feelings?
Should you tell a friend if you see their partner flirting with someone else, even if it might ruin the friendship?
Is it better to have a few deep friendships with high maintenance, or many casual, low-maintenance friendships?
Is it possible to be truly platonic friends with someone you once had feelings for?
Would you rather have $50,000 today tax-free, or $5,000 a year for the next 20 years?
Should basic necessities (food, housing, healthcare) be decommodified, even if it requires a 70% income tax on high earners?
Is buying a home still a smart financial milestone for our generation, or is renting and investing the difference superior?
If you won $10 million, would you tell your family and friends, or keep it a complete secret?
Is it more ethical to donate 10% of your income to charity every year, or accumulate wealth to make a larger impact later?
If a universal basic income of $2,000/month were introduced, would society become more creative or more lazy?
Is debt inherently a tool of financial leverage, or a trap designed to keep the working class subservient?
If an AI could perfectly simulate a deceased loved one using their chat history, would you talk to it?
Will the widespread adoption of AI make humanity smarter by outsourcing rote cognitive work, or dumber by cognitive atrophy?
If a self-driving car must choose between hitting a pedestrian or crashing and killing its passenger, who should it prioritize?
Should we grant legal rights to an artificial general intelligence if it claims to feel pain and have self-awareness?
Is social media ultimately a net positive for global communication, or a net negative for human psychology?
If you could have a chip implanted in your brain that gives you instant access to all internet knowledge but exposes you to potential hacking, would you get it?
Is space exploration a noble pursuit for humanity, or a distraction from solving our problems on Earth?
Does the convenience of smart home devices outweigh the loss of personal privacy to tech corporations?
Would you rather work a job you hate for $300,000 a year, or a job you love for $50,000 a year?
Is the traditional 40-hour work week outdated, or is it the optimal structure for economic and personal stability?
Should managers be elected by their direct reports rather than appointed by executives?
Would you rather be the smartest person in a mediocre company, or the least competent person in a world-class company?
Is it better to specialize deeply in one skill or have a broad but shallow generalist skillset?
If your company developed a product that made your own job obsolete, would you help them implement it?
Is loyalty to an employer a virtuous quality, or a naive misunderstanding of modern capitalism?
A husband steals a drug he cannot afford to save his dying wife. Was his action morally right or wrong?
If you could end all crime permanently by implementing absolute, 24/7 surveillance on all citizens, would you do it?
Is it morally acceptable to consume meat if you are unwilling to personally slaughter the animal?
Should extreme wealth (e.g. billionaires) be outlawed as a policy, or is it a natural incentive for innovation?
If you could press a button that gives you $1 million but randomly kills someone you don't know, would you press it?
Are humans naturally cooperative and good, or naturally selfish and competitive?
Is it worse to fail to do something good, or to actively do something bad?
Should free speech protect the right to express views that are widely considered offensive or harmful?
Would you rather double your intelligence or double your empathy?
If you could travel 100 years into the past or 100 years into the future, which would you choose?
Would you choose to live in a virtual reality where you are perfectly happy and successful, but it's not real?
If you had to lose one of your five senses, which one would you choose to give up?
Is it better to know a lot about a little, or a little about a lot?
If time travel were invented, would it be possible to change the past, or would any action have already happened?
Would you rather live a short, incredibly exciting life (35 years) or a long, quiet, peaceful life (95 years)?
If you could master any one skill instantly, but it meant you could never learn anything else, would you do it?
